metadata.notes: Legacy product, recommend migration to ESP32-C2/C3
Flagship legacy product, extremely popular with hobbyists and in production
WiFi-only variant with USB OTG, good for HMI applications
Most popular current ESP32 variant. AI vector extensions, camera+LCD, USB OTG
Also known as ESP8684. Cost-optimized RISC-V for high-volume IoT
First Espressif RISC-V chip. Popular ESP32 alternative for cost-sensitive applications
First Espressif dual-band WiFi 6 + 802.15.4 tri-protocol SoC. Key differentiation vs competitors.
First ESP with WiFi 6 + BLE 5.3 + 802.15.4 tri-radio. Key Matter/Thread enabler.
Cost-optimized WiFi 6 + BLE variant. Reduced peripheral set vs C6.
No WiFi. Dedicated Thread/Zigbee/BLE mesh node. Low cost for Matter endpoints.
Dual-core RISC-V variant of H-series. BLE 5.4 + 802.15.4. USB OTG and touch sensors.
Most powerful ESP SoC. No built-in radio — pairs with ESP32-C6 for connectivity. MIPI CSI/DSI, USB 2.0 HS, H.264, 400MHz RISC-V.
Enhanced H2 with on-chip DC-DC converter for lower power. RX active 8.2mA, deep sleep 5µA. TX up to 20dBm.
First Espressif WiFi 6E chip. Tri-band 2.4/5/6GHz, 2x2 MIMO, 160MHz BW, up to 2.1Gbps. Connectivity co-processor (not standalone MCU). Uses PCIe/SDIO to connect to host.
